title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Boston Celtics with 27 points, 17 rebounds in a 114-98 win against the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown contributed with 27 points, eight rebounds, and eight assists, showcasing improved passing skills against the 76ers.
Celtics excelled in rebounding, grabbing 59 boards, including 19 offensive, highlighting a strength since early 2026.

title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate rose by 3.5% in 2024, partly due to the Commonwealth v. Sheldon Mattis decision.
Parole rates for life sentence hearings increased from 57% in 2023 to 72% in 2024.
210 individuals were affected by the Mattis decision in 2024, with 144 deemed immediately eligible for parole.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo of Rosalie Miller has been released in efforts to solve her 1997 homicide.
Rosalie Miller was last seen on December 8, 1996, and found dead on January 20, 1997.
Authorities encourage anyone with information about Miller's case to contact the New Hampshire Cold Case Unit.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, is charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
After the shooting, Sousa allegedly caused a fatal car crash in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people on Route 136.
Police found Sousa with a pistol and ammunition; he is in custody at Rhode Island Hospital, expected to survive injuries.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins are interested in trading for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, who has surpassed 80 points each of the last two seasons.
Acquiring Thomas would require Bruins to part with key assets such as draft picks and blue-chip prospects like Dean Letourneau.
St. Louis Blues GM Doug Armstrong may not rush to trade Thomas, who is under a long-term contract, before the deadline.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: The Maine Board of Pesticides Control approved a $3,000 fine for a New York couple over tree poisoning in Rockport.
Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett settled without admitting guilt but acknowledged a court might find them responsible.
Neighbor's son, Eric Grubman, suggested a donation to Maine coastal preservation as amends for the tree poisoning incident.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Several wide receivers impressed at the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine, showcasing potential targets for the Patriots in the draft.
Edge rushers like Romello Height, Dani Dennis-Sutton, and Malachi Lawrence stood out with strong performances at the combine.
Tight end Eli Stowers set a record in the broad jump for his position, marking him as a potential early-round target for the Patriots.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are unlikely to trade major draft capital for a star player this offseason, according to ESPN's Mike Reiss.
New England shows interest in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby, but current trade demands are steep.
Patriots emphasize building through the draft; they hold 11 picks, mostly in later rounds, for the 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power received approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage system on a former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The 16.5-acre facility will feature 816 lithium-ion enclosures with cooling and heating systems and power-conversion infrastructure.
The project is part of a mixed-use development called 'Docklands,' planning additional lab, office, retail, and residential spaces.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on Feb. 28, marking the 13th episode of its 51st season.
Storrie and 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams introduced Mumford & Sons, who were the musical guests.
Members of the US Olympic hockey teams appeared in the monologue, wearing gold medals from the 2026 Winter Olympics.
